app.name: JaundiCare
topbar.subtitle: Newborn jaundice care
dashboard.hero_tag: Early support for mothers and newborns
dashboard.hero_title: Check early. Act early. Stay calm.
dashboard.hero_text: JaundiCare helps parents notice possible newborn jaundice early, understand warning signs, and know when to monitor, recheck, or seek same-day care.
dashboard.create_profile_hint: Create a profile to auto-calculate baby age.
dashboard.check.1: Look at the whites of the eyes.
profile.title: Baby profile
profile.text: Save your baby’s details once. The app will use them to calculate age automatically for screening.
screening.title: Baby screening form
screening.text: If a baby profile is saved, age in hours will be filled automatically.
screening.age_helper: This can be auto-filled from the saved baby profile.
screening.state_helper: Used if location access is unavailable.
screening.image_helper: Use natural light if possible. Focus on the face and eyes.
signs.yellow_eyes: Yellow tinge in the whites of the eyes
result.title: Screening result
result.empty: Submit the form to view the result.
history.title: Screening history
history.text: Review previous screening results and see how decisions changed over time.
education.title: Education
care.title: Care Guide
common.location_unsupported: Geolocation is not supported on this device.
common.location_unavailable: Unable to get location. You can still enter your state manually.
common.request_failed: We could not complete the screening request.
next.urgent.1: Go to a hospital or emergency newborn care service now.
next.urgent.2: Keep feeding the baby if possible while seeking care.
next.urgent.3: Do not delay if the baby is difficult to wake, floppy, or feeding poorly.
next.same_day.1: Arrange same-day review with a doctor, midwife, or health worker.
next.same_day.3: Monitor the eyes, gums, palms, soles, and alertness while waiting for review.
next.recheck.1: Recheck the baby soon, especially in good natural light.
next.recheck.2: Watch for yellow eyes, poor feeding, unusual sleepiness, or spreading yellowing.
next.recheck.3: Seek same-day advice if you remain concerned.
next.monitor.2: Keep monitoring the eyes, gums, palms, soles, and alertness.
next.monitor.3: Seek advice if the baby becomes difficult to wake, feeds poorly, or looks more yellow.
edu.what_is.title: What is jaundice?
edu.what_is.body: Jaundice happens when too much bilirubin builds up in a baby’s body. It can make the eyes, gums, skin, palms, or soles look yellow.
edu.check.title: How to check your baby
edu.check.1: Look at the whites of the eyes.
edu.check.2: Lift the lips and check the gums.
edu.dark_skin.title: Recognising jaundice in Black and Brown babies
edu.dark_skin.body: Jaundice may be harder to see on the skin. Check the eyes, gums, palms, and soles. Watch feeding, sleepiness, and behavior closely.
edu.what_to_do.title: What parents should do
edu.what_to_do.2: Monitor the baby closely in the first days after birth.
edu.what_to_do.3: Use the screening tool when worried.
edu.what_to_do.4: Seek same-day review if warning signs appear.
edu.what_not_to_do.title: What not to do
edu.what_not_to_do.1: Do not ignore poor feeding or unusual sleepiness.
edu.what_not_to_do.2: Do not delay review if you are concerned.
edu.what_not_to_do.3: Do not rely on sunlight alone as treatment.
edu.what_not_to_do.4: Do not assume jaundice is always harmless.
edu.urgent.title: When to seek help urgently
care.feeding.title: Feeding support
care.feeding.body: Frequent feeding helps remove bilirubin from the baby’s body.
care.feeding.3: Seek help early if feeds are being missed
care.mother.title: What mothers can do
care.mother.2: Check eyes and gums in good light
care.mother.3: Ask for help early if something feels wrong
care.maternal.title: Maternal support after delivery
care.maternal.body: Mothers also need recovery support. The goal is to reduce unnecessary prolonged hospital stays by detecting issues early and guiding timely care.
care.nutrition.title: Nutrition reminders for mothers
care.nutrition.3: Do not skip meals while breastfeeding
care.nutrition.4: Rest whenever possible and seek support
chw.title: Community Care Mode
chw.intro: A community health worker workflow for assisted newborn jaundice screening, follow-up reminders, maternal education, and referral support.
chw.actions.title: Quick actions
chw.followup.title: Postnatal follow-up checklist
chw.followup.day1: Day 1: Check feeding, alertness, urine and stool.
chw.followup.day2: Day 2–3: Check eyes, gums, palms and soles for yellowing.
chw.followup.day7: Day 7: Review feeding, weight concern and persistent yellowing.
chw.followup.day14: Day 14: Refer if jaundice is still present or worsening.
chw.referral.title: Referral pathway
chw.risk.title: What CHWs should prioritize
chw.risk.2: Poor feeding with yellowing or unusual sleepiness.
chw.risk.3: Difficulty waking, floppiness, dark urine or pale stool.
chw.risk.4: Darker skin babies where yellowing may be harder to detect on the skin.
chw.alignment.title: Community-based newborn care support
chw.alignment.body: Designed for parents and community health workers, this feature supports early monitoring, structured follow-up, and timely referral when a newborn may need care.
reminder.title: Follow-up reminders
reminder.text: Suggested newborn follow-up reminders based on baby age and jaundice risk.
reminder.empty: Create a baby profile to generate reminders.
reminder.day1: Day 1: Check feeding, alertness, urine and stool.
reminder.day2: Day 2–3: Check eyes, gums, palms and soles for yellowing.
reminder.day7: Day 7: Follow up on feeding, weight concern and yellowing.
reminder.day14: Day 14: Refer if jaundice persists or worsens.
voice.title: Voice guidance
voice.text: Read simple newborn jaundice guidance aloud for parents.
voice.unsupported: Voice guidance is not supported on this browser.
voice.script: Newborn jaundice can be serious if it is not noticed early. Check your baby's eyes, gums, palms and soles for yellowing. Watch feeding, alertness, urine and stool. If your baby is difficult to wake, floppy, feeding poorly, or yellow in the first 24 hours, seek medical help immediately.
trust.title: This is a screening support tool.
trust.body: It does not replace a doctor, midwife, health worker, or bilirubin test.
parent.message.urgent: Take your baby to a hospital now. Do not wait. This result needs immediate medical attention today.
parent.message.same_day: Your baby needs to be seen by a doctor or health worker today. Please do not wait until tomorrow.
parent.message.monitor: Your baby looks okay for now. Keep feeding regularly, watch for yellowing in the eyes and skin, and recheck if anything changes.
urgent_parent: Take your baby to a hospital now. Do not wait. This result needs immediate medical attention today.
same_day_parent: Your baby needs to be seen by a doctor or health worker today. Please do not wait until tomorrow.
recheck_parent: Please recheck your baby soon. Watch for yellowing in the eyes, gums, palms or soles, poor feeding, or unusual sleepiness. Seek same-day advice if you remain concerned.
monitor_parent: Your baby looks okay for now. Keep feeding regularly, watch for yellowing in the eyes and skin, and recheck if anything changes.
amber_uncertain_reason: Symptoms suggest some concern, but the image result was uncertain. A recheck or same-day review is advised.
amber_weak_reason: Symptoms suggest concern and the image result is not strong enough to fully reassure. A recheck or same-day review is advised.
urgent_reason_image: The screening image suggests possible jaundice. Same-day review is advised.
uncertain_reason: The image result was uncertain. Please recheck soon and seek advice if you remain concerned.
weak_normal_reason: The image result is not strong enough to fully reassure. Please monitor closely and seek advice if symptoms appear.
